title: Hercules and the 12 Labors - Review
body:
Daniel takes a look at Hercules and the 12 Labors, a solo dice placement and manipulation game where you step into the sandals of the legendary hero to overcome twelve mythical challenges.

The standout here is the production quality — gorgeous gold foil cards, stunning panoramic artwork, and a beautiful graphic design that punches well above its small-box weight class. Each labour card features lore from the original myths on one side and the challenge mechanics on the other.

What makes it interesting:

  • A clever snowball effect where early rounds feel limited, but as you defeat labours you unlock new dice manipulation powers
  • Multiple win conditions — survive all 12 labours AND max out your divinity track
  • Tough as nails — Daniel admits he’s only made it to labour five so far, and can’t fathom how anyone beats “god mode” with only 3d6

The gameplay loop involves rolling your Hercules dice, using blue powers to manipulate pips, and trying to meet each labour’s specific requirements (matching values, sums, doubles, etc.). Mood cards add variety and occasionally harsh penalties to keep things unpredictable.

Have you tackled Hercules yet? How far have you made it through the labours?
